page: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical Intel Xeon 6975P-C cores, supporting 2 threads per core at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. The system is equipped with 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core. There is no local storage or GPU bundled with this instance, and baseline network bandwidth is rated at 1 Gbps.

Benchmark results show strong single-core capabilities, with Geekbench single-core scoring 2110.0 and single-threaded gzip compression reaching 34.08 GB/sec. Text processing performance is highly competitive, placing in the top 10% with a score of 2789.0. However, multi-core and memory-bound workloads exhibit moderate to weak performance. The multi-core Geekbench score is 4866.0, and the stress-ng multi-core CPU performance is in the bottom 25% at 4301.36. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is also relatively weak at 321.45 GB/sec, though large block uncached read and write bandwidths maintain average tier performance at 40.9 GB/sec and 28.5 GB/sec respectively.

The c8i-flex.xlarge offers a cost-effective entry point for compute-focused workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local storage. Its advanced instruction set support, including AVX-512 and Intel AMX for matrix multiplication, enhances its utility for lightweight machine learning tasks, such as small-model LLM inference prompt processing which achieves 506.14 tokens/sec. Given its strong single-threaded performance and moderate multi-core capacity, this server is well-suited for single-threaded applications, text processing, static web serving, and basic database operations.

It features 4 dedicated vCPUs from 2 physical Intel Xeon 6975P-C cores running at 3.9 GHz, paired with 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory. The server lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on a 1 Gbps baseline network. Performance benchmarks reveal strong single-core execution, highlighted by a top-tier text processing score and strong single-threaded compression speeds. Conversely, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics fall into average or weak categories. Supported by advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, the instance provides a cost-efficient profile for compute-heavy, single-threaded applications, static web serving, and lightweight machine learning inference.

8.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 7 GHz
